FirmwareUpdater.sourceSignature: class FirmwareUpdater { state(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; snapshot(): FirmwareUpdateState; refreshPrerequisites(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; selectOemTarget(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; admitLocalCustomTarget(targetValue: LocalCustomFirmwareTarget, artifact: AdmittedFirmwareArtifact): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; download(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; prepare(input: FirmwareUpdatePreflight): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; detectDfu(): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState>; flash(input: FirmwareFlashRequest): Promise<FirmwareUpdateState> }

AdmittedFirmwareArtifact.sourceSignature: export interface AdmittedFirmwareArtifact { readonly targetId: string; openVerified(): Promise<VerifiedFirmwareArtifact>; }

VerifiedFirmwareArtifact.sourceSignature: export interface VerifiedFirmwareArtifact { readonly descriptor: number; readonly bytes: Uint8Array; assertStable(): Promise<void>; close(): Promise<void>; }

invocations.inputDescription: A composed updater whose durable evidence has not yet been recovered.
invocations.outputDescription: Recovered legal v1/v2 history projected into current v2 state and synchronized with the device.

errors.description: Evidence is conflicting, malformed, orphaned, locked, or lacks a process-local custom artifact capability.

invariants: Started, completed-unverified, and indeterminate sessions never become writable.
Historical v1 OEM evidence remains readable but current writes persist v2 target identity.

A recovered prepared local-custom target remains fail-closed until the exact openVerified descriptor capability is rebound; successful rebind durably demotes to awaiting-dfu, clears prior DFU identity, and requires fresh detection.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.state owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.outputDescription: Current strict cloned state without I/O.

errors.description: Internal state violates the runtime schema.

invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.snapshot owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: A recovered updater, optionally with a prepared session.
invocations.outputDescription: State with freshly inspected dfu-util availability.

errors.description: No exact dfu-util 0.11 prerequisite is available.

invariants: Inspection is read-only and persists ready-state demotion when needed.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.refreshPrerequisites owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: An unprepared updater whose write disposition is not-started.
invocations.outputDescription: State targeting the canonical OEM release.

errors.description: Preparation or a write boundary locks target selection.

invariants: Target selection discards artifacts belonging to another target.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.selectOemTarget owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: A strict manifest-derived target plus its bound AdmittedFirmwareArtifact capability.
invocations.outputDescription: State with a reverified app-owned custom target artifact.

errors.description: Target, artifact binding, size, digest, or manifest proof fails.

invariants: AdmittedFirmwareArtifact targetId equals the selected target and openVerified returns reverified bytes bound to one open descriptor.
Qualified targets require qualificationEvidenceSha256; unqualified targets forbid it.
Custom target selection never uses the OEM network fetch path.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.admitLocalCustomTarget owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: An eligible updater with the OEM target selected.

errors.description: Transport, bounded read, digest, or create-once install failed.

invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.download owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: Strict physical and human attestations for the selected target.
invocations.outputDescription: Durably journaled v2 preparation and safely disconnected CDC session.

errors.description: Observation, artifact reread, persistence, or safe disconnect failed.

invariants: Target ID/SHA, battery, device ID, USB continuity, screen hash, image hash, and attestations bind to one preparation UUID.
Target selection is immutable after preparation.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.prepare owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.outputDescription: Ready-to-flash only with one canonical DFU identity.

errors.description: Tool or physical DFU target admission failed.

invariants: Detected identity fingerprint is persisted before confirmation.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.detectDfu owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

invocations.inputDescription: Exact preparation UUID plus target-kind-specific native-only confirmation literal.
invocations.outputDescription: Post-reboot verified completion or fail-closed state.

errors.description: The write boundary was crossed without complete verification.

invariants: Write owner lock and v2 started journal are durable before dfu-util spawn.
Completion is archived only after exact device ID and selected target version/revision/qualification verification.
Ordinary observation of custom version/revision cannot prove exact custom target content; only the just-completed durable transaction may retain exact-target completion evidence.
invocations.validationBoundary: src/core/firmware-updater.ts#FirmwareUpdater.flash owns the exact declared call boundary; referenced runtime validators apply before untrusted values are used.

structuralCapabilities.outputDescription: Fresh verified bytes and the exact open descriptor inherited by dfu-util.

errors.description: Descriptor metadata or fresh bytes no longer match the selected target.

invariants: No firmware pathname is persisted or passed to dfu-util; assertStable runs immediately before spawn and close runs after child exit or failure.
structuralCapabilities.validationBoundary: This is a non-invocable structural relationship; each referenced value is validated at the receiving source capability identified by this contract.
